Multi-Page Layout Test

Demonstrating Proper Page Breaks

December 12, 2025

Test Document

Generated: December 12, 2025 | Confidential Document
LOGO
Page 2 of 10
Introduction

Purpose of This Test

This test document demonstrates proper multi-page layout techniques using HTML and CSS. The goal is to create a document that displays correctly both on screen (with visual separation between pages) and when printed (with proper page breaks).

Each page is exactly 8.5 inches wide by 11 inches tall, which is the standard US Letter size. The pages include 0.75 inch margins on all sides to ensure content is safely within the printable area of most printers.

Key Techniques Used

The CSS employs separate media queries for screen and print viewing. For screen display, pages are shown with a 20px gap and drop shadow to clearly indicate page boundaries. For print, pages use the page-break-after property to ensure each page starts on a new sheet of paper.

Content is prevented from overflowing page boundaries using the overflow: hidden property on screen, and page-break-inside: avoid on print to prevent awkward content splits.

Testing Methodology

To properly test this layout, view this document in a web browser and use Print Preview to see how it will appear when printed. Each page should start at the top of a new sheet, and content should not overflow or get cut off unexpectedly.

Generated: December 12, 2025 | Confidential Document
LOGO
Page 3 of 10
Visual Content

Image Handling

Images and other visual elements must be carefully sized to fit within page boundaries. This page demonstrates how to include images that don't cause layout issues or overflow onto adjacent pages.

Image Placeholder
200px height

The image above is sized to fit comfortably within the page margins while leaving room for surrounding text content. In a real document, you would replace this placeholder with actual images using the img tag.

It's important to set explicit width and height constraints on images to prevent them from causing unexpected page breaks or layout issues. Using max-width: 100% ensures images scale down on smaller displays while respecting their container boundaries.

Generated: December 12, 2025 | Confidential Document
LOGO
Page 4 of 10
Tabular Data

Data Tables

Tables present a particular challenge in multi-page layouts. They must be carefully sized to fit within page boundaries, or broken across pages in a way that maintains readability.

Item Description Value Status
Item 1 First test item for demonstration $1,234 Active
Item 2 Second test item for demonstration $2,345 Pending
Item 3 Third test item for demonstration $3,456 Active
Item 4 Fourth test item for demonstration $4,567 Complete
Item 5 Fifth test item for demonstration $5,678 Active

The table above demonstrates proper formatting with alternating row colors and clear column headers. In print mode, the page-break-inside: avoid property helps prevent tables from breaking at awkward positions.

Generated: December 12, 2025 | Confidential Document
LOGO
Page 5 of 10
Extended Content

Paragraph Flow

This page tests how longer text content behaves within the page boundaries. The key is ensuring that text flows naturally without overflowing the bottom margin of the page. Each page has a fixed height of 11 inches, with 0.75 inch margins, leaving 9.5 inches of usable vertical space.

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip ex ea commodo consequat. Excepteur sint occaecat cupidatat non proident, sunt in culpa qui officia deserunt mollit anim id est laborum. Sed ut perspiciatis unde omnis iste natus error sit voluptatem accusantium doloremque laudantium, totam rem aperiam, eaque ipsa quae ab illo inventore veritatis et quasi architecto beatae vitae dicta sunt explicabo.

Content Density

The amount of content that fits on a single page depends on font size, line height, and margin settings. This example uses Arial font at standard size with 1.6 line height for comfortable reading. Adjusting these values will change how much content fits per page.

Nemo enim ipsam voluptatem quia voluptas sit aspernatur aut odit aut fugit, sed quia consequuntur magni dolores eos qui ratione voluptatem sequi nesciunt. Neque porro quisquam est, qui dolorem ipsum quia dolor sit amet, consectetur, adipisci velit.

Generated: December 12, 2025 | Confidential Document
LOGO
Page 6 of 10
Mixed Layout

Combining Elements

Real-world documents often combine different types of content on the same page. This page demonstrates how text, lists, and other elements can coexist while respecting page boundaries.

Best Practices

  • Always set explicit page dimensions (8.5in x 11in)
  • Use consistent margins on all pages (0.75in recommended)
  • Apply page-break-after: always for print media
  • Use overflow: hidden to prevent content spillover
  • Test in print preview before finalizing
  • Include page numbers for easy reference
  • Add headers and footers for context
  • Use page-break-inside: avoid for content blocks

Common Pitfalls

The most common mistakes in multi-page layouts include forgetting to set fixed page heights, not using proper page-break properties, and failing to test in print preview. Always validate your layout before deploying to production.

Generated: December 12, 2025 | Confidential Document
LOGO
Page 7 of 10
Visual Data

Chart Placeholder

Data visualizations like charts and graphs need special consideration in paginated layouts. They should be sized to fit entirely within a single page to maintain readability.

Chart/Graph Placeholder
300px height

The visualization above demonstrates a larger content block that still fits comfortably within the page boundaries. When working with actual charts, libraries like Chart.js or D3.js should be configured with explicit dimensions that respect the available space.

Responsive Considerations

While this layout is optimized for printing and PDF generation, it's also important to consider how it displays on different screen sizes. The fixed 8.5in width means users on smaller screens will need to zoom out or scroll horizontally.

Generated: December 12, 2025 | Confidential Document
LOGO
Page 8 of 10
Financial Summary

Quarterly Results

Financial reports often require multiple tables across several pages. Each table should be self-contained and properly formatted for easy reading.

Quarter Revenue Expenses Net Income
Q1 2025 $125,000 $75,000 $50,000
Q2 2025 $135,000 $78,000 $57,000
Q3 2025 $145,000 $82,000 $63,000
Q4 2025 $155,000 $85,000 $70,000
Total $560,000 $320,000 $240,000

The table above includes a highlighted total row to draw attention to summary data. This is achieved through inline styles that override the default alternating row colors.

Generated: December 12, 2025 | Confidential Document
LOGO
Page 9 of 10
Technical Implementation

CSS Media Queries

The core of this layout system relies on two media queries: @media screen and @media print. The screen query adds visual spacing and shadows to separate pages, while the print query ensures proper pagination.

Page Break Properties

Modern CSS provides several page break properties:

  • page-break-after: Forces a break after the element
  • page-break-before: Forces a break before the element
  • page-break-inside: Controls breaks within the element
  • break-after: Modern replacement for page-break-after
  • break-before: Modern replacement for page-break-before
  • break-inside: Modern replacement for page-break-inside

Browser Compatibility

This layout is tested and works correctly in all modern browsers including Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge. The older page-break properties are included alongside newer break properties for maximum compatibility.

PDF Generation

When converting to PDF using browser print or libraries like Puppeteer, these page breaks are properly respected, resulting in a clean multi-page PDF document.

Generated: December 12, 2025 | Confidential Document
LOGO
Page 10 of 10
Conclusion

Summary of Techniques

This test document has demonstrated all the essential techniques for creating proper multi-page layouts in HTML and CSS:

  • Fixed page dimensions (8.5in x 11in)
  • Consistent margins (0.75in all sides)
  • Screen vs print media queries
  • Proper page break properties
  • Content overflow prevention
  • Headers and footers on each page
  • Page numbering
  • Mixed content types (text, images, tables)

Testing Checklist

When implementing this in your own documents, verify the following:

  • Pages display with visual separation in browser
  • Print preview shows 10 separate pages
  • No content overflows page boundaries
  • Headers appear on every page
  • Page numbers are sequential
  • Tables don't break awkwardly
  • Images fit within page margins
  • PDF export produces clean results

Next Steps

Use this test file as a reference when debugging issues in your report templates. Compare the CSS and HTML structure to identify what's causing incorrect page breaks or layout problems in your production code.
